data.title: BP Gets Earnings Bump as Oil Traders Capitalize on War-Induced Volatility
data.description:
(06:00 GMT) *BP PLC 1Q EPS 24.53c
(06:00 GMT) *BP PLC 1Q Net Pft $3.84B
(06:00 GMT) *BP PLC 1Q Rev $53.37B
(06:00 GMT) *BP PLC 1Q Sales $52.26B
(06:00 GMT) *BP PLC 1Q Pretax Pft $7.37B
(06:00 GMT) *BP PLC 1Q EBIT $8.49B
(06:01 GMT) *BP 1Q Replacement Cost Profit $662M
(06:02 GMT) *BP 1Q Underlying RC Profit $3.2B
(06:02 GMT) *Analysts Saw BP Underlying RC Profit at $2.67B
(06:04 GMT) *BP Declares Dividend of 8.32c
(06:15 GMT) BP Gets Earnings Bump as Oil Traders Capitalize on War-Induced Volatility

By Adam Whittaker

BP's quarterly earnings more than doubled as its oil traders capitalized on market volatility triggered by the conflict in the Middle East.

The British oil major reported an underlying replacement cost profit--a similar metric to net income that U.S. oil companies report--of $3.2 billion for the first quarter of the year. This compares with the $1.54 billion it reported in the fourth quarter of last year and the $2.67 billion analysts had expected, according to a company-compiled consensus.

BP's customers and products division, which houses its oil trading unit, reported quarterly earnings of $2.5 billion, compared with $1.4 billion in the prior quarter, amid major oil-price swings. BP doesn't break out its trading performance when publishing results but the company described the unit's trading performance as "exceptional."

The company has launched a turnaround program which is now being led by recently-appointed CEO Meg O'Neill. It is simplifying its portfolio and selling some assets with the aim of cutting debt and improving profitability.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: CARD: Revenue up 7.4%, Adjusted PBT down 15.2%, strong cash flow enables £15m buyback and higher dividend
data.description:
Revenue rose 7.4% to £582.7m, driven by acquisitions and new stores, but Adjusted PBT fell 15.2% to £56.0m due to weaker UK footfall. Strong cash flow supports a 5.0p dividend and a £15m share buyback, with FY27 guidance in line with consensus.

Original document: Card Factory Plc [CARD] Annual Financial Statement — Apr. 28 2026

data.title: Bank Of Japan Keeps Rate Unchanged
data.description:
TOKYO (dpa-AFX) - The Bank of Japan left its key interest rate unchanged on Tuesday and upgraded its inflation outlook citing higher global crude oil prices.

The policy board voted 6-3 majority to hold the uncollateralized overnight call rate at around 0.75 percent.

The bank had raised its interest rate by 25 basis points to the current level in December 2025.

In response to rising crude oil prices, the bank significantly raised its inflation projections. Inflation outlook for the fiscal 2026 was lifted to 2.8 percent from 1.9 percent, and the projection for the fiscal 2027 was raised to 2.3 percent from 2.0 percent. Inflation is forecast to reach 2.0 percent in the fiscal 2028.

'...the Bank will continue to raise the policy interest rate and adjust the degree of monetary accommodation, in response to developments in economic activity and prices as well as financial conditions,' the bank said in a statement.

data.title: Georgia Capital Q1 NAV per share flat in GEL terms
data.description:
Overview

  • Georgia investment holding firm's Q1 NAV per share was flat in GEL terms, up slightly in GBP

  • Private portfolio companies' revenue and EBITDA grew by 13.7% and 26.9% yr/yr, respectively

  • Company repurchased 475,000 shares and launched a new US$ 50 mln buyback program

Outlook

  • Company expects continued robust NAV per share growth, driven by portfolio company performance

  • Company says Georgia's macroeconomic environment remains robust, with GDP growth expected to moderate

  • Company expects inflation to remain elevated in first half of 2026 due to geopolitical pressures

Result Drivers

  • PRIVATE PORTFOLIO GROWTH - Strong revenue and EBITDA growth at large private portfolio companies drove value creation, with retail, healthcare, and insurance businesses all posting double-digit gains

  • RENEWABLE ENERGY WRITE-DOWN - Value creation was partially offset by a one-off write-down in the renewable energy business after discontinuing two pipeline projects

  • LISTED PORTFOLIO DECLINE - Listed portfolio value fell due to GEL appreciation against GBP and a largely flat Lion Finance Group share price

data.title: Anglo American Q1 copper output rises, iron ore drops; back FY output view
data.description:
Overview

  • UK-based diversified miner's Q1 copper output rose 1% yr/yr, premium iron ore fell 2%

  • Manganese ore production more than doubled, rebounding from prior-year cyclone disruption

  • Rough diamond output up 17%, but average realized diamond price fell 19% amid weak demand

Outlook

  • Anglo American maintains 2026 copper production guidance at 700-760 kt, unit cost c.172 c/lb

  • Company maintains 2026 premium iron ore guidance at 55-59 Mt, unit cost c.$41/tonne

  • De Beers 2026 diamond production guidance unchanged at 21-26 mln carats, unit cost c.$80/carat

Result Drivers

  • COPPER OUTPUT - Higher throughput at Los Bronces and Collahuasi drove copper production, offsetting lower grades at Quellaveco

  • IRON ORE SLIP - Premium iron ore production fell 2%, with Kumba impacted by lower Kolomela output and planned stock drawdown, partly offset by higher Sishen production

  • DIAMONDS - Rough diamond production rose 17% on planned ore release and higher volumes from Venetia underground and Gahcho Kué, but realized prices fell 19% amid weak demand and a shift to lower-value goods

Company press release:

data.description:
Overview

  • Sweden audiobook platform's Q1 net sales rose 3% yr/yr, with 7.9% growth at CER

  • Adjusted EBITDA margin improved to 17.0% from 14.1%, reflecting higher profitability

  • Q1 net profit and EPS rose sharply, supported by subscriber growth and strong cash flow

Outlook

data.title: Gold hits three-week low with US-Iran talks, central bank decisions in focus
data.description:
Key points:
  • Trump unhappy with latest Iran proposal, official says
  • Dollar, oil gain on stalled U.S.-Iran peace talks
  • Fed expected to hold rates steady on Wednesday

By Noel John

Gold fell to a three-week low on Tuesday, as elevated oil prices kept inflation concerns high, while investors awaited key central bank decisions this week to see if the Middle East conflict has altered the interest rate outlook.

Spot gold was down 1.1% at $4,628.88 per ounce, as of 0553 GMT, its lowest level since April 7. U.S. gold futures for June delivery fell 1.1% to $4,643.70.

U.S. President Donald Trump is unhappy with the latest Iranian proposal on resolving the two-month war, a U.S. official said, dampening hopes for a resolution to the conflict that has disrupted energy supplies, fuelled inflation, and killed thousands.

"Geopolitical headlines are still the main driver (of gold prices). In the event of a deal (between the U.S. and Iran) or an interim deal, the dollar should weaken, and gold will likely break out to the upside," said Edward Meir, analyst at Marex.

The dollar edged higher, and oil prices hovered above $110 a barrel, as the crucial Strait of Hormuz waterway remained largely shut.

Higher crude oil prices can stoke inflation by raising transportation and production costs, increasing the likelihood of higher interest rates. While gold is considered an inflation hedge, high interest rates make yield-bearing assets more attractive, weighing on its appeal.

The U.S. Federal Reserve is widely expected to hold interest rates steady at the end of its two-day meeting on Wednesday.

"The Fed is going to be largely irrelevant. They're not going to be doing anything on rates for now," said Meir, adding that it might cut rates later in the fourth quarter as "we're heading into a global slowdown."

Investors will also be focusing on other central bank decisions this week, including those from the European Central Bank, the Bank of England and the Bank of Canada.

Spot silver fell 3% to $73.23 per ounce, platinum lost 1.5% to $1,953.50, and palladium was down 2.1% at $1,445.50.
